etymology.description: The name 毛澤東(毛泽东) (Máo Zédōng) refers specifically to the historical figure Mao Zedong, a founding father of the People's Republic of China. This name is composed of three characters: 毛(毛) (máo, "hair; surname"), 澤(泽) (zé, "marsh; favor; grace"), and 東(东) (dōng, "east"). In this context, 毛(Máo) serves as a family or clan name without inherent meaning about Mao Zedong's leadership or ideology. 澤(Zé) commonly refers to grace or kindness, while 東(Dōng) indicates direction, but here they form part of a personal name rather than conveying specific attributes of meaning.
etymology.interpretation: While 毛(毛) (máo, "hair; surname") provides no inherent semantic in this context, it is a common Chinese surname. 澤(澤) (zé, "marsh; favor; grace") could historically imply beneficence or nurturing qualities, but here it's merely a namesake. 東(東) (dōng, "east") likely signifies geographical or directional symbolism in some names, but in Mao Zedong's case, it is a standard component of the given name with no primary interpretation. Thus, Mao Zedong's name doesn't imply a synthesis of these elements but identifies a historical personality.

examples.chinese: 毛泽东(毛澤東)是中国革命的重要领袖(是中國革命的重要領袖)。
examples.pinyin: Máo Zédōng shì Zhōngguó gémìng de zhòngyào lǐngxiù.
examples.english: Mao Zedong was an important leader of the Chinese revolution.

examples.chinese: 毛泽东(毛澤東)于1949年建立了中华人民共和国(於1949年建立了中華人民共和國)。
examples.pinyin: Máo Zédōng yú 1949 nián jiànlì le Zhōnghuá Rénmín Gònghéguó.
examples.english: Mao Zedong established the People's Republic of China in 1949.

examples.chinese: 许多人认为毛泽东(許多人認為毛澤東)对20世纪的中国影响深远(對20世紀的中國影響深遠)。
examples.pinyin: Xǔduō rén rènwéi Máo Zédōng duì èrshí shìjì de Zhōngguó yǐngxiǎng shēnyuǎn.
examples.english: Many people believe Mao Zedong had a profound influence on 20th-century China.
